question: A camp counselor has 54 paintbrushes, 72 tubes of paint, and 90 sheets of paper to distribute to the children in the camp. If each child receives an equal number of each item and there are no items remaining, what is the greatest possible number of children in the camp?

solution.strategy: The greatest number of children is the GCF of the three quantities.

question: A teacher has 24 pencils, 36 erasers, and 60 sheets of sticker paper to give to students. If each student receives an equal number of each item and there are no items remaining, what is the greatest possible number of students?

solution.strategy: The greatest number of students is the GCF of the three quantities.

question: A coach has 48 water bottles, 64 energy bars, and 80 towels to distribute to the team. If each team member receives an equal number of each item and there are no items remaining, what is the greatest possible number of team members?

solution.strategy: The greatest number of team members is the GCF of the three quantities.

question: A scout leader has 30 compasses, 45 flashlights, and 75 first-aid kits to give to scouts. If each scout receives an equal number of each item and there are no items remaining, what is the greatest possible number of scouts?
